creator: hlh@restart.be
text:
The problem crop up:

[root@norquay ~]# pfctl -ss|wc -l
     533

[root@norquay ~]# procstat -kk 7
  PID    TID COMM                TDNAME              KSTACK                       
    7 100084 pf purge            -                   mi_switch+0x118 sleepq_timedwait+0x40 _sleep+0x268 pf_purge_thread+0xec fork_exit+0x94 

[root@norquay dtrace]# ./pf.dtrace 
dtrace: script './pf.dtrace' matched 4 probes
dtrace: buffer size lowered to 2m

after:

[root@norquay ~]# echo "set timeout interval 10" | pfctl -mf -

And then no new state are created!

[root@norquay ~]# nohup service pf restart

resume the normal work of pf.
